(born 15 November 1986) is an English male international [lawn and indoor bowler](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

**Jamie Chestney** (born 15 November 1986) is an English male international [lawn and indoor bowler](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Bowls career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jamie_Chestney&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Bowls career")]

### International achievements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jamie_Chestney&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International achievements")]

His first major international medal came in 2011 when he won the singles and triples gold medals at the [Atlantic Bowls Championships](/wiki/Atlantic_Bowls_Championships "Atlantic Bowls Championships").[[2]](#cite_note-2) The following year he gained a [bronze medal](/wiki/Bronze_medal "Bronze medal") at the [2012 World Outdoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2012_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship "2012 World Outdoor Bowls Championship") in the [pairs competition](/wiki/2012_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_Pairs "2012 World Outdoor Bowls Championship – Men's Pairs") with [Graham Shadwell](/wiki/Graham_Shadwell "Graham Shadwell").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Two years later he competed for England in the [men's fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2014_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2014 Commonwealth Games – Men's fours") at the [2014 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2014_Commonwealth_Games "2014 Commonwealth Games") where he won a [silver medal](/wiki/Silver_medal "Silver medal").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

He finished runner-up in the 2016 [International Open](/wiki/World_Bowls_Tour_Events "World Bowls Tour Events") and the following year won the Cooperative Funeralcare International Open.

In 2018, he won the [World Indoor Open Pairs title](/wiki/2018_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2018 World Indoor Bowls Championship") with [Mark Dawes](/wiki/Mark_Dawes "Mark Dawes")[[5]](#cite_note-5) and then won the Mixed Pairs title the following day with [Lesley Doig](/wiki/Lesley_Doig "Lesley Doig").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In 2018 he was also selected as part of the [English team](/wiki/England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "England at the 2018 Commonwealth Games") for the [2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2018_Commonwealth_Games "2018 Commonwealth Games") on the [Gold Coast](/wiki/Gold_Coast,_Queensland "Gold Coast, Queensland") in [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland")[[7]](#cite_note-7) where he claimed a [bronze medal](/wiki/Bronze_medal "Bronze medal") in the [Fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2018 Commonwealth Games – Men's fours") with [David Bolt](/wiki/David_Bolt_(bowls) "David Bolt (bowls)"), [Louis Ridout](/wiki/Louis_Ridout "Louis Ridout") and [Sam Tolchard](/wiki/Sam_Tolchard "Sam Tolchard").[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Chestney won a third world indoor title when winning the open pairs at the [2021 World Indoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2021_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2021 World Indoor Bowls Championship") for the second time with Mark Dawes.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

In 2022, he competed in the [men's triples](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_triples "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Men's triples") and the [men's fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Men's fours") at the [2022 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2022_Commonwealth_Games "2022 Commonwealth Games").[[12]](#cite_note-12) The team of Chestney, [Nick Brett](/wiki/Nick_Brett "Nick Brett") and [Louis Ridout](/wiki/Louis_Ridout "Louis Ridout") won the triples gold medal and in the fours he also secured a bronze medal.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

### National achievements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jamie_Chestney&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: National achievements")]

Chestney is a four times national champion, winning [National Championship](/wiki/List_of_Bowls_England_Men%27s_National_Championships "List of Bowls England Men's National Championships") titles in 2004, 2009, 2021 and 2025.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) Chestney was aged just 17 when he won the pairs title with Ian Wynter in 2004.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

He was part of the Kings Bowls Club, who have won the [Top Club championship](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Top_Club) "Bowls England National Championships (Top Club)") four years running from 2016-2019.[[17]](#cite_note-17) He then joined the Culm Vale club.

**National titles**

* 2004 [Bowls England National Championships (men's pairs)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(men%27s_pairs) "Bowls England National Championships (men's pairs)")
* 2009 [Bowls England National Championships (Men's Junior Singles)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Men%27s_Junior_Singles) "Bowls England National Championships (Men's Junior Singles)")
* 2021 [Bowls England National Championships (Men's Champion of Champions)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Men%27s_Champion_of_Champions) "Bowls England National Championships (Men's Champion of Champions)")
* 2025 [Bowls England National Championships (men's triples)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(men%27s_triples) "Bowls England National Championships (men's triples)")

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jamie_Chestney&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In 2015, he married fellow bowls international [Natalie Melmore](/wiki/Natalie_Melmore "Natalie Melmore").[[18]](#cite_note-18) In 2020, he joined the [Bowls England](/wiki/Bowls_England "Bowls England") staff as a its Membership Register Project Officer.
